capabilities.hero:
Voice that thinks.
At the speed of a heartbeat.
capabilities.lead: Sub-second latency. 46 languages. Function calling, RAG, and real-time speech detection — the substrate every Finn agent runs on.
capabilities.pillarsHeading:
Five primitives.
Every conversation built on top.
capabilities.pillarsLead: The model layer is a commodity. The infrastructure around it isn't — and that's where Finn earns the call.

pillars.title: Sub-second voice loop
pillars.body: Streaming ASR + LLM + TTS in a single hot pipeline. No turn-by-turn lag. Customers hear a human cadence, not a robot.

pillars.title: Function calling, in-call
pillars.body: Pull from your CRM, fire webhooks, run business logic mid-conversation. The agent never says 'one moment please.'

pillars.title: Multilingual by default
pillars.body: Switch language mid-call. Hindi to English to Tamil — same agent, same voice persona, same context.

pillars.title: Knowledge base + RAG
pillars.body: Upload PDFs, sitemaps, FAQs. Finn grounds every answer in your data. No hallucinated SLAs.

pillars.title: Voice library
pillars.body: 100+ voices across accents and languages. Clone your own brand voice in under a minute.

pillars.title: Real-time speech detection
pillars.body: Interrupt handling, silence detection, voicemail discrimination — built in, not bolted on.

capabilities.closingHeading:
Built for the real world.
Not the demo.
capabilities.closingBody: Every component above is benchmarked on production traffic — 50M+ minutes shipped, 250+ peak concurrent calls, customers on five continents.

sections.body: Most stacks chain providers in series and pay the latency tax at every hop. Finn runs streaming speech-to-text, model inference, and text-to-speech in one fused pipeline — sub-400ms end-to-end. Customers hear a real cadence, not a stop-and-wait.

observability.lead: Recordings, transcripts, sentiment, outcomes — captured automatically. Spot drift, prove ROI, ship improvements in days not quarters.
observability.pillarsHeading:
See what happened.
Fix what didn't.
observability.pillarsLead: Voice ops without observability is hope-driven engineering. Finn ships the entire instrumentation layer with the platform.

pillars.title: Auto-transcribed calls
pillars.body: Every conversation captured, indexed, and searchable. Speaker diarisation, timestamps, redactions for PII.

pillars.title: Post-call analysis (PCA)
pillars.body: Outcome tags, intent extraction, sentiment, dispute flags — generated automatically per call. No human in the loop.

pillars.title: Built-in eval suite
pillars.body: Score every call against your custom rubric. Adherence, tone, accuracy, compliance — track them call-over-call.

pillars.title: Drift detection
pillars.body: Catch the silent regression. We alert when model behaviour shifts, vocabulary changes, or outcomes slide.

pillars.title: Live conversation playback
pillars.body: Audio sync'd with transcript and PCA highlights. Replay any moment, share with one click.

pillars.title: Real-time dashboards
pillars.body: Today's outcomes, sentiment heatmap, agent-level performance — live, not yesterday's.

observability.closingHeading:
You can't improve
what you can't see.
observability.closingBody: Finn doesn't just run the calls. It tells you which ones converted, which ones leaked, and what to change tomorrow.

sections.heading: Auto-transcribed. Speaker-diarised. Searchable.
sections.body: The first job of observability is full coverage. Finn captures every audio call, generates a speaker-aware transcript, redacts PII, and indexes the lot — usable in under sixty seconds from call hang-up.

sections.heading: Outcomes, sentiment, and intent — auto-tagged.
sections.body: Every call ships with a structured outcome record. Sentiment delta, intent classification, dispute flags, hand-off reasons — generated by the model, not by human QA. Wire it into your CRM and act tomorrow.
bullets: Sentiment scored per turn + per call
PCA payload pushed via webhook to your stack

sections.heading: Score the agent against your rubric. Continuously.
sections.body: Build a custom eval suite — adherence, tone, accuracy, compliance — and Finn scores every call. Drift detection alerts when behaviour shifts. Closed-loop feedback into prompt + workflow updates.
bullets: Human feedback feeds back into the system

orchestration.lead: Drag-and-drop call flows, conditional branching, human handoff, multi-agent coordination — orchestration that survives the production messy real world.
orchestration.pillarsHeading:
Compose any call flow.
In minutes.
orchestration.pillarsLead: The hard part of voice AI isn't the AI — it's the workflow around it. Routing, escalation, fallback, state. Finn ships them all.

pillars.title: Drag-and-drop workflow editor
pillars.body: Visual call flows. Variables, branches, loops, transfers — every node configurable, no JSON spelunking.

pillars.title: Conditional routing
pillars.body: Route by intent, sentiment, audience segment, time of day. Send VIPs to live agents, others to self-serve.

pillars.title: Live human handoff
pillars.body: Warm transfer with full context — the human picks up where Finn left off. No 'can you repeat your account number?'

pillars.title: Multi-agent coordination
pillars.body: Specialist agents for specialist tasks. Triage → Booking → Confirmation, all under one orchestrator.

pillars.title: Voicemail + fallback
pillars.body: Detect voicemail, drop tailored messages. Failed call? Auto-retry with backoff. Unreachable? Queue for tomorrow.

pillars.title: Variables + memory
pillars.body: Persist caller state across nodes and across calls. Build genuinely stateful conversations.

orchestration.closingBody: Real customers say weird things, hang up mid-sentence, call back twice. Finn's orchestration was designed against that — not for happy paths.

sections.heading: Drag a node, ship a flow. No code spelunking.
sections.body: The workflow editor exposes every primitive — say, branch, capture, function call, transfer, hang-up — as draggable nodes. Variables persist across nodes. Conditional routing splits the path. Whatever business logic runs in your head, it runs in the editor.

sections.body: One agent for triage. Another for booking. A third for confirmation. Stitch them together with an orchestrator that hands state between them — same call, same caller, escalating context.

security.lead: SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, ISO 27001 — compliance isn't a checkbox, it's how the platform was designed. From key management to data residency.
security.pillarsHeading:
Compliance is
the floor, not the ceiling.
security.pillarsLead: Voice is regulated. Healthcare, finance, insurance, legal — Finn was built to run there from day one.

pillars.title: End-to-end encryption
pillars.body: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit. Recordings, transcripts, PII — all encrypted by default.

pillars.title: Data residency
pillars.body: Pick where your data lives. US, EU, India regions available — no data leaves the chosen geography.

pillars.title: Audit trails
pillars.body: Every API call, every config change, every data access — logged, immutable, exportable.

pillars.title: PII redaction
pillars.body: Auto-redact card numbers, SSNs, account IDs from transcripts. Custom regex for industry-specific patterns.

pillars.title: Role-based access
pillars.body: Granular permissions per user, per workspace. SSO via SAML or OIDC. SCIM provisioning for enterprise.

pillars.title: Compliance frameworks
pillars.body: SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA BAAs, GDPR DPA, ISO 27001 — documents available under NDA in under 24 hours.

security.closingBody: Security is what you ship, not what you say. Every Finn deployment carries the same controls the regulated incumbents use — without their procurement cycle.

sections.heading: End-to-end. Defense in depth.
sections.body: AES-256 at rest. TLS 1.3 in transit. Per-workspace KMS keys. SSO via SAML or OIDC. SCIM provisioning. Role-based access control granular down to the field level.

workflow-editor.hero:
Drag a node.
Ship a flow.
workflow-editor.lead: Visual call flow editor — say, capture, branch, function call, transfer. No code spelunking. Variables persist across nodes and across calls.
workflow-editor.closingHeading:
From idea to live call,
in minutes.
workflow-editor.closingBody: Every node is configurable. Every connection conditional. Build the flow your business actually runs — not the demo's happy path.
workflow-editor.howHeading:
Three steps from
blank canvas to live call.

howSteps.title: Drop nodes on the canvas
howSteps.body: Say, capture, branch, function call, transfer — every primitive available as a draggable node. Connect them with arrows.

howSteps.title: Wire conditional logic
howSteps.body: Branch on intent, captured values, sentiment, time of day. Variables persist across nodes and across calls.

howSteps.title: Deploy to a phone number
howSteps.body: Hot-deploy without dropping calls in flight. Versioned + reversible — roll back in one click if the new flow misbehaves.

workflow-editor.benefitsHeading:
Everything the workflow needs,
in the editor.

benefits.title: Hot deploy + rollback
benefits.body: Push changes without dropping live calls. Roll back to any prior version in one click.

benefits.title: Variables that travel
benefits.body: Captured fields persist across nodes, calls, and agents. No state stitching.

benefits.title: Conditional everything
benefits.body: Branch on intent, captured value, sentiment, hours, geography — any signal the call exposes.

benefits.title: Diff view per change
benefits.body: See exactly what changed between versions. Audit-ready, no spelunking through commits.

faqs.question: Do I need to write code?
faqs.answer: No. The workflow editor is a visual canvas — drag nodes, connect them with arrows, configure each node in a side panel. Function-call nodes optionally take JSON schemas, but the editor never asks you to write a line of code.

faqs.question: What happens to in-flight calls when I deploy a new version?
faqs.answer: In-flight calls finish on the version they started on. New calls start on the latest deploy. No dropped calls, no surprised callers.

faqs.question: Can I roll back if a new version misbehaves?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every save creates a versioned snapshot. Roll back to any prior version with one click — the diff view shows you exactly what changed.

faqs.question: How are variables shared between nodes?
faqs.answer: Anything captured by a `capture` node is available to every downstream node in the same call. Persistent variables (caller name, last-call context) survive across calls and across agents.

faqs.question: Does the editor support conditional branching?
faqs.answer: Yes — branch on intent, captured values, sentiment, time of day, audience segment, or any custom signal exposed in your workflow. Branches can route to different sub-flows or transfer to specific human queues.

knowledge-base.lead: Upload PDFs, sitemaps, FAQs, internal docs. Finn embeds, chunks, and retrieves the right passage every turn. Source citations attached to every answer.
knowledge-base.closingHeading:
No hallucinated SLAs.
No made-up policies.
knowledge-base.closingBody: Knowledge base grounds every response in your actual data. If the answer isn't there, Finn says so — and routes the caller to someone who knows.
knowledge-base.howHeading:
From your docs
to grounded answers.

howSteps.title: Upload anything
howSteps.body: PDFs, sitemaps, FAQ pages, public APIs, Notion exports — drop them in. Auto-detected source type, no schema config.

howSteps.title: Chunk + embed + index
howSteps.body: Smart chunking respects document structure. Re-embeds on source change. Per-Finn workspace isolation — your agent never sees another team's data.

howSteps.title: Retrieve + cite mid-call
howSteps.body: Sub-200ms retrieval per turn. Citations attached to every answer so QA can audit the source. If the answer isn't there, Finn says so.

knowledge-base.benefitsHeading:
Every answer
traceable to a source.

benefits.title: Source citations on every reply
benefits.body: QA and compliance can audit any answer back to the exact passage. No black-box responses.

benefits.title: Auto re-indexing on change
benefits.body: Update a PDF, push a doc change — the knowledge base re-indexes. No stale answers.

benefits.title: Per-Finn workspace isolation
benefits.body: Sales finn never sees support docs. HR never sees billing PII. Strict separation per agent.

benefits.title: Refuses to hallucinate
benefits.body: If the answer isn't in the corpus, Finn says so — and routes the caller to someone who knows.

faqs.question: What document formats are supported?
faqs.answer: PDFs, Markdown, HTML pages, sitemaps, Notion exports, Google Docs, public REST endpoints. Anything text-based — we extract, chunk, and embed automatically.

faqs.question: How fast is retrieval mid-call?
faqs.answer: Sub-200ms per query in the steady state. Retrieval runs in parallel with LLM inference so it doesn't add latency to the voice loop.

faqs.question: What happens when a source document changes?
faqs.answer: Sources are polled on a schedule (default daily, configurable). Changed documents are re-chunked and re-embedded automatically. In-flight calls finish on the prior version.

faqs.question: Can different Finns share or isolate knowledge bases?
faqs.answer: Both. You can scope a knowledge base to one Finn, share it across a team, or attach multiple knowledge bases to a single Finn with priority weighting.

faqs.question: What if the answer isn't in the knowledge base?
faqs.answer: Finn says so and either escalates to a human, takes a callback request, or routes to a different specialist agent — your choice, configured per Finn.

voice-library.lead: Pick a stock voice or clone your own in 60 seconds. Switch language mid-call. Tune accent, pace, pronunciation per finn.
voice-library.closingHeading:
Sound like you.
Not a robot.
voice-library.closingBody: Brand voice cloning from a 60-second sample. Custom pronunciation for SKUs, names, and jargon. Preview every voice before deploying.
voice-library.howHeading:
Pick a voice.
Or clone yours.

howSteps.title: Browse the library
howSteps.body: 100+ stock voices across 46 languages. Filter by accent, age, tone, gender. Preview every voice with a real sample before assigning.

howSteps.title: Clone in 60 seconds
howSteps.body: Upload a 60-second sample of your spokesperson, agent, or brand voice. Cloned voice ready to deploy. Consent + watermarking built in.

howSteps.title: Tune per Finn
howSteps.body: Adjust pace, pitch, pause length, accent strength. Add custom pronunciations for SKUs, drug names, technical jargon. Per-Finn voice profile.

voice-library.benefitsHeading:
Tuned for trust.
Not for novelty.

benefits.title: Sub-second TTS
benefits.body: Streaming synthesis — speech starts before the LLM finishes generating. No awkward dead air mid-response.

benefits.title: Cloned with consent
benefits.body: Voice cloning requires written consent + audio watermarking. Compliant with EU AI Act + state biometric laws.

benefits.title: Custom pronunciation library
benefits.body: Override how Finn says any word. SKUs, drug names, addresses, brand terms — all rendered the way you want.

benefits.title: Mid-call language switch
benefits.body: Caller responds in Spanish? Finn switches mid-turn. No re-routing, no second agent.

faqs.question: How long does voice cloning take?
faqs.answer: 60 seconds of clean audio + a written consent form = a cloned voice ready to deploy within 5 minutes. Quality scales with sample length; we recommend 2-3 minutes for production use.

faqs.question: What languages are supported?
faqs.answer: 46 languages including English (multiple accents), Spanish, Mandarin, Hindi, Arabic, Portuguese, French, German, Japanese, Korean, and most major European + Asian languages. Real-time accent + dialect handling.

faqs.question: Can Finn switch languages mid-call?
faqs.answer: Yes. Caller can respond in any supported language and Finn detects + switches automatically. No re-routing or second agent required.

faqs.question: How do I add custom pronunciations?
faqs.answer: Add words to a per-Finn pronunciation dictionary. Specify the spelling and the phonetic or audio sample you want Finn to render. Useful for SKUs, drug names, technical jargon.

faqs.question: What's the latency for voice synthesis?
faqs.answer: Streaming TTS starts speech within ~100ms of the LLM beginning to generate. Full sub-second voice loop end-to-end.

live-transfer.lead: When the call needs a human, Finn doesn't dump the caller on a cold queue. It hands off the summary, the captured fields, and the next-step intent.
live-transfer.closingHeading:
No 'can you repeat
your account number?'
live-transfer.closingBody: The human picks up where Finn left off — with the call summary, the structured outcome, and the captured fields already loaded.

howSteps.title: Detect the moment
howSteps.body: Caller asks for a human, sentiment drops, intent maps to a high-touch path, or your business rules say escalate. Finn surfaces the signal in real time.

howSteps.title: Match to the right human
howSteps.body: Skill-based routing picks the right queue, agent, or rep. Falls back to voicemail or scheduled callback when no one's available.

howSteps.title: Warm handoff with context
howSteps.body: Human picks up with the call summary, captured fields, sentiment timeline, and proposed next step already on screen. Caller never repeats themselves.

benefits.title: Skill-based routing
benefits.body: Route to the right specialist by language, tier, queue, or any custom signal. Not just round-robin.

benefits.title: Voicemail + callback fallback
benefits.body: When no human's available, Finn books a callback or takes a voicemail. No dropped calls.

benefits.title: Whisper coaching
benefits.body: Optional: human gets a 5-second audio summary in their ear before the caller comes through.

benefits.title: Outcome captured on handoff
benefits.body: Structured outcome + captured fields pushed to your CRM the moment the transfer completes.

faqs.question: What carriers support live transfer?
faqs.answer: Twilio, Plivo, Telnyx, plus SIP trunk-based BYOC. Most US, EU, and APAC carriers are supported via these providers.

faqs.question: How does the human get the context?
faqs.answer: On transfer pickup, the human sees a one-page summary: caller name, intent, captured fields, sentiment timeline, and Finn's proposed next step. Pushed via screen-pop into your existing CRM or helpdesk.

faqs.question: What happens if no one's available to take the call?
faqs.answer: Finn books a callback at the caller's preferred time, takes a voicemail, or escalates to a different queue based on your fallback rules.

faqs.question: Can Finn transfer to an external phone number?
faqs.answer: Yes — Finn can warm-transfer to any phone number (your call center, an on-call rep, a partner). The handoff includes a verbal context summary spoken to the receiver before the caller is bridged in.

faqs.question: Does the caller hear hold music during transfer?
faqs.answer: Finn keeps the caller engaged with a short status update ("Connecting you with a senior specialist now…") rather than dumping them on dead air or generic hold music.

post-call-analysis.lead: Outcome, sentiment, intent, dispute flags — generated automatically per call. No human QA required. Wire it into your CRM, act tomorrow.
post-call-analysis.closingBody: PCA tags every call against your custom outcome taxonomy. Sentiment per turn. Dispute auto-flagged. Pushed to your stack on call end.
post-call-analysis.howHeading:
Tag the call.
Act on the outcome.

howSteps.title: Define your outcome taxonomy
howSteps.body: Pick from templates (sales qualified, payment confirmed, dispute raised) or define your own. Per-workflow taxonomy with mutually exclusive labels.

howSteps.title: Auto-tag on call end
howSteps.body: PCA runs the moment the call disconnects. Outcome, sentiment per turn, intent, captured fields, and dispute flags — all generated automatically.

howSteps.title: Push to your stack
howSteps.body: Webhook fires with structured payload to your CRM, helpdesk, data warehouse, or Slack channel. Act on the outcome before the rep finishes their next call.

post-call-analysis.benefitsHeading:
From hours of
listening to zero.

benefits.title: 100% call coverage
benefits.body: Every call tagged. Not a 5% sample. Not the obvious wins. The escalations, the no-shows, the disputes — all surfaced.

benefits.title: Custom taxonomy per workflow
benefits.body: Sales workflow tags differently than collections. Define outcomes that match how your team thinks about the work.

benefits.title: Sentiment per turn
benefits.body: Spot the exact moment a call went sideways. Coach reps on what worked, drop the prompts that didn't.

benefits.title: Dispute auto-flag
benefits.body: Caller raises a TCPA, GDPR, or service complaint — PCA flags + freezes the call for compliance review.

faqs.question: How quickly does PCA run after call end?
faqs.answer: Within 30 seconds of disconnect. Outcome, sentiment, transcript, and structured fields all available via webhook + dashboard within that window.

faqs.question: Can I customize the outcome taxonomy?
faqs.answer: Yes. Define your own mutually exclusive outcomes per workflow. We provide templates for common verticals (sales, support, collections, healthcare) as starting points.

faqs.question: Where do PCA results get sent?
faqs.answer: Anywhere you have a webhook endpoint — CRM, helpdesk, data warehouse, Slack, Linear, your own internal API. Multiple destinations per workflow supported.

faqs.question: Is the full transcript stored?
faqs.answer: Yes. Full word-level transcript with speaker diarization, timestamps, and confidence scores. Stored per your data retention policy.

faqs.question: Can I disable PCA on sensitive workflows?
faqs.answer: Yes. PCA can be disabled per workflow or per call (e.g. HIPAA-restricted flows). Captured fields and outcome can be redacted before storage.

batch-calling.lead: Upload an audience CSV, pick a workflow, hit go. Smart retry, DNC scrubbing, time-zone-aware dialing, concurrency throttling — all built in.
batch-calling.closingHeading:
Reach every contact.
In their window.
batch-calling.closingBody: From 100 calls to 100,000 — same workflow, same orchestrator, same observability. Pause, resume, throttle from one panel.

howSteps.title: Upload an audience
howSteps.body: Drop a CSV, push via API, or sync from your CRM. We auto-map columns to variables and validate phone formats + DNC status on import.

howSteps.title: Pick a workflow
howSteps.body: Attach any workflow from the editor — sales qualification, payment reminder, appointment confirmation, survey. Same workflow scales from 100 to 100,000 calls.

howSteps.title: Throttle, pause, observe live
howSteps.body: Real-time dashboard shows answered / no-answer / voicemail / busy. Pause the campaign instantly, adjust concurrency, drill into individual call transcripts.

benefits.title: Smart retry + backoff
benefits.body: No-answer + busy lines get retried with exponential backoff and time-of-day adjustments. Voicemail-detected calls drop without burning a retry.

benefits.title: DNC scrub on every dial
benefits.body: National + state DNC lists checked before every connect. Per-account opt-out lists synced in real time. TCPA-safe by default.

benefits.title: Time-zone-aware windows
benefits.body: Dial only during legal calling windows in the contact's timezone. Configurable per state for stricter regimes (e.g. CA, MA).

benefits.title: Concurrency throttling
benefits.body: Cap how many calls run in parallel — from 5 to 5,000. Avoid blowing up your downstream CRM or compliance limits.

faqs.question: What's the maximum campaign size?
faqs.answer: No hard cap. Customers run 50,000-call campaigns regularly; we've ramped to 250,000-call days on enterprise plans. Concurrency is what gets sized, not total contacts.

faqs.question: How does Finn handle voicemail?
faqs.answer: Voicemail detection is built in. You configure per-workflow whether to leave a message, drop the call silently, or schedule a retry. Detection latency is ~1.2s.

faqs.question: Is DNC scrubbing automatic?
faqs.answer: Yes. National + state DNC lists are checked on every dial. Per-account opt-out lists sync in real time. Calls to scrubbed numbers are skipped (not retried).

faqs.question: Can I pause a campaign mid-run?
faqs.answer: Yes — one click. In-flight calls finish, queued calls pause. Resume, adjust concurrency, or cancel from the same panel.

faqs.question: How is timezone determined for each contact?
faqs.answer: Auto-detected from area code by default, overrideable via a CSV column or CRM field. Dial windows respect state-level calling-window laws (CA 8a-9p, MA 8a-8p, etc.).

branded-call-id.hero:
Your brand name.
On the caller ID.
branded-call-id.lead: Pickup rates jump 30-60% when the caller sees a verified business name instead of an unknown number. Branded Caller ID gets you that lift on every dial.
branded-call-id.closingBody: Branded Caller ID surfaces your name, logo, and call reason on supported carriers. STIR/SHAKEN attested. Spam-likely labels avoided. More pickups, fewer wasted dials.
branded-call-id.howHeading:
Verify your brand.
Land on every screen.

howSteps.title: Verify your business
howSteps.body: One-time registration with the call-trust analytics providers (TNS, Hiya, First Orion). We handle the paperwork — you provide the EIN, domain, and brand assets.

howSteps.title: Carrier registration
howSteps.body: Your verified name + logo + call reason get pushed to T-Mobile, AT&T, Verizon, and global carrier networks. Updates propagate within 24-48 hours.

howSteps.title: Track pickup lift
howSteps.body: Dashboard surfaces pickup-rate delta vs unbranded calls per region + carrier. A/B test brand presentation per campaign.

benefits.title: STIR/SHAKEN A-attestation
benefits.body: Highest attestation level — carriers trust your call origination. No 'Scam Likely' tag on T-Mobile, AT&T, or Verizon.

benefits.title: Logo + call reason
benefits.body: On supported handsets (iOS 17+, Pixel, Samsung), caller sees your logo + a one-line reason for the call. Built-in trust signal.

benefits.title: Reputation monitoring
benefits.body: We monitor your number's reputation across analytics providers + flag spam-labeling proactively. Auto-remediation built in.

benefits.title: Multi-region carrier coverage
benefits.body: US (T-Mobile, AT&T, Verizon), UK, EU, India, Australia carrier networks. Coverage expands monthly.

faqs.question: How long does verification take?
faqs.answer: Typically 3-5 business days end-to-end. We handle the paperwork; you provide EIN, business address, domain, and brand assets (logo, call reasons).

faqs.question: What's the typical pickup lift?
faqs.answer: 30-60% lift on US calls within 30 days, depending on industry and prior reputation. Healthcare and financial services see the highest delta because of pre-existing spam labeling.

faqs.question: Which carriers and handsets support branded display?
faqs.answer: T-Mobile, AT&T, Verizon, US Cellular, plus most major global carriers. Display surfaces on iOS 17+, Pixel devices, and Samsung One UI 5+. Older handsets see the verified name without the logo.

faqs.question: What if my number gets flagged as 'Spam Likely'?
faqs.answer: We monitor your reputation across the three major analytics providers (TNS, Hiya, First Orion) and remediate flagging automatically. Most cases clear within 48 hours.

faqs.question: Does this work for outbound campaigns or only single calls?
faqs.answer: Both. Branded Caller ID is per-number — every dial from a verified number surfaces the brand. Pairs naturally with Batch Calling for outbound campaigns.

sms-follow-up.hero:
SMS that lands
before the caller hangs up.
sms-follow-up.lead: Finn fires the right text mid-call or right after — confirmation, payment link, document, calendar invite. Templated, personalized, delivered.
sms-follow-up.closingBody: SMS is a node in the workflow editor, not a separate tool. Same orchestrator, same delivery logs, same compliance posture.
sms-follow-up.howHeading:
Trigger from any node.
Delivered in seconds.

howSteps.title: Drop an SMS node in the flow
howSteps.body: Same drag-and-drop editor. SMS sits next to capture, branch, transfer. Configure body, sender ID, link tracking in a side panel.

howSteps.title: Merge caller fields into the body
howSteps.body: Reference any captured variable — name, amount, slot, link. Templates are versioned + previewable so the rep sees exactly what the caller gets.

howSteps.title: Deliver + track
howSteps.body: Sub-2-second delivery via your carrier. Status (delivered, read, clicked) writes back to the call timeline + your CRM.

benefits.title: Mid-call delivery
benefits.body: Send while the caller is still on the line — payment link, address, document. No 'I'll text you later.'

benefits.title: TCPA-safe consent
benefits.body: Verbal consent captured + logged before any outbound SMS. Opt-out keywords (STOP, UNSUBSCRIBE) auto-honored across the account.

benefits.title: Per-workflow templates
benefits.body: Sales template, support template, payment template — each versioned, previewable, audit-logged.

benefits.title: Link tracking + write-back
benefits.body: Short links per recipient. Click + delivery events fire webhooks to your CRM in real time.

faqs.question: Can Finn send SMS during a live call?
faqs.answer: Yes. SMS nodes can fire mid-call — typical use cases are payment links, calendar invites, document delivery, or 2FA codes the caller needs in the same session.

faqs.question: How does TCPA + consent get handled?
faqs.answer: Verbal consent is captured + logged before any outbound SMS. STOP / UNSUBSCRIBE replies auto-suppress the recipient across the entire account, not just the workflow.

faqs.question: Which carriers and countries are supported?
faqs.answer: US + Canada (Twilio, Plivo, Telnyx), UK, EU, India, Australia. Long codes, short codes, and toll-free messaging supported. 10DLC registration handled for US sending.

faqs.question: Can I personalize per recipient?
faqs.answer: Yes. Any captured variable can be merged into the SMS body — caller name, amount, slot, custom fields from your CRM. Templates support conditional blocks (`{{if hasAccount}}…{{/if}}`).

faqs.question: Where do delivery + click events show up?
faqs.answer: On the call timeline in the dashboard + via webhook to your CRM or data warehouse. Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, and custom webhooks all first-class.

whatsapp-follow-up.hero:
WhatsApp messages,
from the same workflow.
whatsapp-follow-up.lead: Verified business profile. Rich media. Templated post-call follow-up with documents, payment links, and booking confirmations.
whatsapp-follow-up.closingHeading:
WhatsApp is the inbox.
Meet them there.
whatsapp-follow-up.closingBody: 85% read rate within 5 minutes. Triggered from any call workflow with full variable merging + audit-logged consent.
whatsapp-follow-up.howHeading:
Verified profile.
Approved templates. Live in days.

howSteps.title: Onboard your business profile
howSteps.body: WhatsApp Business API verification — we handle Meta paperwork. Verified green-tick profile with your logo and call-back number.

howSteps.title: Approve message templates
howSteps.body: Pre-approve templates per use case (confirmation, reminder, payment, support). Approval cycle ~24h. Variable slots stay flexible.

howSteps.title: Fire from any call node
howSteps.body: WhatsApp node in the editor next to SMS. Mid-call or post-call. Rich media (PDF, image, location) attached automatically based on context.

benefits.title: 85% read rate
benefits.body: WhatsApp messages get opened — within 5 minutes typically. Compare to ~20% for email.

benefits.title: Rich media attachments
benefits.body: PDF invoices, location pins, booking confirmations, product images. Sent natively in the conversation.

benefits.title: Two-way conversation handoff
benefits.body: Customer replies route back to a human or a chat agent. No dead-end one-way blasts.

benefits.title: Multi-language templates
benefits.body: Approve templates in 46 languages. Sent in the caller's preferred language automatically.

faqs.question: How long does WhatsApp Business onboarding take?
faqs.answer: Typically 3-5 business days for Meta verification + green-tick. We handle the paperwork; you provide business documents (incorporation cert, address, domain, logo).

faqs.question: Why do templates need pre-approval?
faqs.answer: WhatsApp requires templates for any business-initiated outbound message (utility, marketing, authentication). Approval is one-time per template; ~24h turnaround from Meta.

faqs.question: Can I attach files?
faqs.answer: Yes. PDFs, images, videos, audio, location pins, contact cards. Attached natively to the WhatsApp message — no external link.

faqs.question: What happens when the customer replies?
faqs.answer: Replies route to a human chat agent, a Finn chat workflow, or your existing helpdesk. No dead-end blasts — two-way conversation supported by default.

faqs.question: Is opt-in required?
faqs.answer: Yes. WhatsApp requires opt-in before the first outbound business message. Finn captures + logs verbal consent during the call before triggering the first WhatsApp send.

email-follow-up.lead: Templated emails fired from any call node. Variables merged, attachments wired, delivery + open + click logged back to the call.
email-follow-up.closingHeading:
Same call, same thread.
Same audit trail.
email-follow-up.closingBody: Email triggered from the workflow lands in the call timeline. Sales reps see what was sent. Compliance sees what was logged.
email-follow-up.howHeading:
Fire from any node.
Authenticated, tracked, attached.

howSteps.title: Author + version templates
howSteps.body: HTML or plain text. Variables merged from captured fields. Per-workflow template library. Preview every email before deploying.

howSteps.title: Wire attachments
howSteps.body: Attach PDFs, contracts, invoices — generated on the fly from the call's structured data. Or pick from your document library.

howSteps.title: Send + track
howSteps.body: Sent from your verified domain with SPF/DKIM/DMARC. Delivery, open, click, reply — all events fire back to the call timeline + your CRM.

email-follow-up.benefitsHeading:
Lands in the inbox.
Not in spam.

benefits.title: From your domain, authenticated
benefits.body: SPF, DKIM, DMARC configured on setup. Mails come from yourcompany.com, not a generic shared domain.

benefits.title: Conditional bodies
benefits.body: Different templates fire based on call outcome — sale-closed vs callback-scheduled get different copy.

benefits.title: Reply routing
benefits.body: Replies route to the right human, queue, or CRM record. Tied back to the original call thread.

benefits.title: Bounce + suppression management
benefits.body: Hard bounces auto-suppress. Soft bounces retry with backoff. Compliance-grade unsubscribe handling.

faqs.question: Can Finn send email during a live call?
faqs.answer: Yes. Email nodes can fire mid-call — typical use cases are sending a contract, invoice, calendar invite, or document the caller needs to see in the same session.

faqs.question: How is deliverability handled?
faqs.answer: Sent from your verified domain with SPF, DKIM, and DMARC configured. We manage IP warming, suppression lists, and bounce handling. Typical inbox placement >98%.

faqs.question: Can I attach generated documents?
faqs.answer: Yes. PDFs, contracts, invoices, and receipts can be generated from the call's structured data and attached automatically. Or pick from a pre-uploaded document library.

faqs.question: Where do open + click events show up?
faqs.answer: On the call timeline in the dashboard, plus webhooks to your CRM / data warehouse on every event (delivered, opened, clicked, replied, bounced, unsubscribed).

faqs.question: How are replies handled?
faqs.answer: Replies route to a human inbox, a Finn chat workflow, or your existing CRM record. Threading is preserved so the conversation stays tied to the original call.

telegram-follow-up.hero:
Telegram bots,
for calls that need a chat.
telegram-follow-up.lead: Trigger Telegram messages from any call node. Reach customers in markets where Telegram is the dominant channel — India, Brazil, MENA, Eastern Europe.
telegram-follow-up.closingHeading:
Telegram in India.
WhatsApp in LATAM. Done.
telegram-follow-up.closingBody: Wire Telegram alongside SMS, WhatsApp, and email. Pick channels per region or per persona. Same orchestrator, same compliance posture.

howSteps.title: Connect your Telegram bot
howSteps.body: BotFather token in 30 seconds. Or let us provision a dedicated bot tied to your brand profile.

howSteps.title: Drop Telegram nodes
howSteps.body: Same workflow editor as SMS + WhatsApp + email. Configure body, attachments, inline buttons, polls.

howSteps.title: Two-way conversation handoff
howSteps.body: User replies to the bot route to a human agent or a Finn chat workflow. Conversation threaded to the originating call.

benefits.title: 700M+ active users globally
benefits.body: Dominant chat platform in India, Brazil, Russia, Iran, parts of MENA + Eastern Europe. Reach customers SMS misses.

benefits.title: Inline buttons + polls
benefits.body: Send polls, quick-reply buttons, web app launches — interactive surfaces SMS can't match.

benefits.title: Rich media + files up to 2GB
benefits.body: Send PDFs, videos, location, audio — natively in the chat. No external link fallback.

benefits.title: Free at any scale
benefits.body: Telegram Bot API has no per-message cost. Run 1,000 or 1,000,000 messages — same flat platform fee.

faqs.question: Why use Telegram over SMS?
faqs.answer: Telegram is the dominant chat channel in many markets (India, Russia, Iran, Brazil, parts of MENA + Eastern Europe). Free at any volume, supports rich media, polls, inline buttons, files up to 2GB. SMS still wins for universal reach in US/EU.

faqs.question: Do I need my own bot or do you provide one?
faqs.answer: Both options. Bring your own BotFather token in 30 seconds, or let us provision a dedicated bot tied to your verified brand profile (custom name, avatar, description).

faqs.question: Can users start a conversation with the bot?
faqs.answer: Yes. Inbound Telegram messages route to a Finn chat workflow, a human agent, or your existing helpdesk — same handoff logic as voice.

faqs.question: What's the cost compared to WhatsApp?
faqs.answer: Telegram Bot API has no per-message charge. WhatsApp charges per conversation window (utility, marketing, authentication tiers). Telegram wins on cost at high volume in supported regions.

faqs.question: Can I send polls and inline buttons?
faqs.answer: Yes. Telegram's interactive surfaces (inline keyboards, polls, quizzes, web app buttons) are first-class node options in the workflow editor.

voicemail-detection.lead: Finn distinguishes human pickup from machine in under 1.5 seconds. Drop silently, leave a templated message, or schedule a retry — your choice, per workflow.
voicemail-detection.closingBody: Voicemail detection runs on every outbound dial. Configurable behavior per campaign. No more half-cooked messages mid-greeting.

howSteps.title: Real-time audio classification
howSteps.body: Acoustic + cadence model detects machine greetings in the first ~1.2 seconds. Tuned across US + international voicemail systems.

howSteps.title: Pick the policy
howSteps.body: Drop silently (preserve retry budget), leave a templated message after the beep, or mark for callback scheduling.

howSteps.title: Log + adjust
howSteps.body: Per-call detection event logged. Aggregate dashboard shows machine-rate per campaign so you can tune dial windows + retry logic.

benefits.title: Sub-1.5s detection
benefits.body: Decision made before the greeting finishes — no awkward 'hello hello hello' from Finn.

benefits.title: Beep-aware delivery
benefits.body: When leaving a message, Finn waits for the beep — no clipped intros.

benefits.title: Configurable per workflow
benefits.body: Sales campaigns drop voicemail. Collections leave a compliance-required notice. Same engine.

benefits.title: Retry-budget aware
benefits.body: Drops don't burn a retry. Beeped messages do. All policy-driven.

faqs.question: How accurate is voicemail detection?
faqs.answer: ~97% accuracy in the steady state across US + major international carriers. Continuously retrained on edge cases (carrier-specific greetings, foreign-language voicemail systems).

faqs.question: What's the detection latency?
faqs.answer: Under 1.5 seconds in 95% of cases. Finn won't speak until the classification settles, so the caller never hears overlapping greetings.

faqs.question: Can I leave different messages per campaign?
faqs.answer: Yes — per-workflow templates with variable merging. Sales template, collections template, appointment-reminder template, all separately versioned.

faqs.question: What happens when detection is uncertain?
faqs.answer: Configurable per workflow — default is to treat as human (better caller experience). Can be flipped to skip+retry for high-volume outbound.

faqs.question: Does this affect retry budgets?
faqs.answer: Silent drops don't consume a retry. Voicemails left after the beep mark the contact as 'reached' so they don't get re-called the same day.

realtime-interruption.lead: Natural turn-taking — when the caller speaks over Finn, Finn stops mid-word. No talking over each other. No awkward pauses.
realtime-interruption.closingHeading:
Yields like a human.
Not a kiosk.
realtime-interruption.closingBody: Real-time barge-in detection — Finn drops the floor the instant the caller speaks. No more 'press 1 to interrupt.'

howSteps.title: Bidirectional audio stream
howSteps.body: Finn's mic stays open during synthesis. VAD detects caller voice the instant they start.

howSteps.title: Yield mid-word
howSteps.body: Under 80ms from detected voice to TTS stop. No mid-sentence collisions.

howSteps.title: Resume context-aware
howSteps.body: Acknowledges interruption, processes what the caller said, picks up the right thread. No 'as I was saying.'

benefits.title: Sub-80ms yield
benefits.body: Faster than human reaction time. Finn drops the floor before the caller registers they're talking over.

benefits.title: Clean resume
benefits.body: Tracks what was said + what was cut off. Acknowledges + adapts. No robotic 'one moment please.'

benefits.title: Tunable barge-in threshold
benefits.body: Per-workflow VAD sensitivity. Quiet caller in a quiet room vs busy contact center — both work.

benefits.title: False-positive resistant
benefits.body: Trained to distinguish caller voice from ambient noise, music, cross-talk. Finn doesn't yield to a sneeze.

faqs.question: What's the technical latency for barge-in?
faqs.answer: Sub-80ms from detected voice onset to TTS halt. Faster than the caller can perceive their own interruption.

faqs.question: How does Finn handle the interruption content?
faqs.answer: Captures what the caller said, processes it against the conversation context, and responds appropriately — apology, clarification, branch to new topic, whatever fits.

faqs.question: Can I tune sensitivity per workflow?
faqs.answer: Yes. Quiet inbound support calls use a low threshold. Noisy outbound to mobile users uses a higher threshold to ignore ambient noise.

faqs.question: What about cross-talk on bad connections?
faqs.answer: Echo cancellation + caller-voice classification filter cross-talk + line noise. Finn doesn't yield to its own voice bouncing back.

faqs.question: Does this work across all telephony providers?
faqs.answer: Yes — Twilio, Plivo, Telnyx, BYOC SIP trunk. Requires bidirectional streaming audio, which all major providers support.

mid-call-language-switch.hero:
Caller switches language.
Finn switches too.
mid-call-language-switch.lead: Detect language at every turn. Switch synthesis + transcription mid-call. No re-routing, no second agent, no awkward 'hold while I transfer you.'
mid-call-language-switch.closingBody: Mid-call language switch keeps the conversation natural. Captured fields, intent, sentiment — all preserved across language transitions.

howSteps.title: Per-turn language detection
howSteps.body: Every caller turn classified for language. Sub-200ms classification overhead.

howSteps.title: Hot-swap TTS + ASR
howSteps.body: Synthesis + transcription models swap mid-conversation. Voice persona preserved across languages (when supported).

howSteps.title: Continue with full context
howSteps.body: Captured fields, intent, sentiment, workflow position all preserved. No restart, no apology.

benefits.title: 46 languages supported
benefits.body: Major European, Asian, MENA, Latin American languages. New languages added monthly.

benefits.title: Same voice across languages
benefits.body: Cloned voices that span multiple languages keep the same persona. Caller doesn't hear a new agent.

benefits.title: Workflow-agnostic
benefits.body: Any workflow inherits multilingual support. Per-finn configuration if you want to lock to one language.

benefits.title: Captured fields translated on capture
benefits.body: Caller answers in Spanish, Finn captures the structured value in your workflow's canonical language.

faqs.question: Which languages are supported?
faqs.answer: 46 languages including English (multiple accents), Spanish, Mandarin, Hindi, Tamil, Arabic, Portuguese, French, German, Japanese, Korean, and most major European + Asian languages.

faqs.question: How quickly does the switch happen?
faqs.answer: Mid-sentence — typically <300ms from detected language change to first response in the new language. Caller experiences it as natural.

faqs.question: Is the conversation context preserved?
faqs.answer: Yes — captured fields, intent, sentiment, workflow position all carry across the language switch. No restart, no 'let me get my colleague.'

faqs.question: Can I lock a Finn to one language?
faqs.answer: Yes. Per-finn configuration if your business operates in one language only. Default is auto-detect across all 46.

faqs.question: Does the voice change with the language?
faqs.answer: Cloned voices that support multiple languages (most of our library) keep the same persona across all of them. Stock voices may switch to a native speaker for that language.

silence-handling.lead: Configurable silence policies — gentle prompt after 3s, second prompt after 6s, graceful exit after 10s. Per-workflow tuning. No more dead air.
silence-handling.closingBody: Smart silence handling fills the gap without nagging. Different policies for capture turns (be patient) vs decision turns (re-prompt fast).

howSteps.title: Detect silence
howSteps.body: VAD measures speech absence per turn. Threshold tunable — quieter inbound vs noisy mobile outbound.

howSteps.title: Tiered prompts
howSteps.body: First gentle prompt at 3s ('Are you still there?'). Second at 6s ('I can call back if now isn't good.'). Graceful exit at 10s.

howSteps.title: Log + classify
howSteps.body: Silence events tagged on the call timeline + PCA output. Helps spot bad audio paths, distracted callers, system issues.

silence-handling.benefitsHeading:
Patience where it pays.
Prompts where it doesn't.

benefits.title: Per-turn-type timers
benefits.body: Capture turns (caller thinking) get 6s. Decision turns (yes/no) get 3s. Configured per workflow.

benefits.title: Three-tier prompts
benefits.body: Soft → medium → graceful exit. Caller never feels rushed or stranded.

benefits.title: Custom prompt phrases
benefits.body: Default English phrases included. Replace with your brand voice + tone, per language.

benefits.title: Audit-friendly
benefits.body: Every silence event tagged so you can spot bad audio routes or recurring caller-side issues.

faqs.question: What are the default silence timers?
faqs.answer: 3s gentle prompt → 6s second prompt → 10s graceful exit with retry-callback offer. All adjustable per workflow + per turn type.

faqs.question: Can I customize the prompt phrases?
faqs.answer: Yes — replace defaults with your brand voice + tone. Different phrases per language, per workflow.

faqs.question: How is silence different from a pause?
faqs.answer: Pauses are <500ms (natural turn-taking). Silence kicks in only when speech is absent past the configured threshold for the current turn type.

faqs.question: Does silence handling work during interruption?
faqs.answer: Yes — if the caller interrupts then goes quiet mid-thought, Finn waits the configured threshold before re-prompting, not just barreling forward.

faqs.question: Are silence events logged?
faqs.answer: Yes — every silence trigger tagged on the call timeline + included in PCA output. Useful for spotting bad audio paths or recurring caller-side issues.

custom-pronunciation.lead: Per-finn pronunciation dictionary. Override any word — SKUs, drug names, technical jargon, brand terms — with phonetic spelling or audio sample.
custom-pronunciation.closingBody: Build a pronunciation dictionary in minutes. Audio samples for tricky words. Phonetic for the rest. Versioned + audit-logged.
custom-pronunciation.howHeading:
Add a word.
Finn never says it wrong again.

howSteps.title: Add via dashboard or API
howSteps.body: Paste the misspoken word. Provide a phonetic spelling (IPA or ARPAbet) or upload a 1-second audio sample.

howSteps.title: Scope it
howSteps.body: Per-finn (only one agent uses it) or per-account (all finns share). Versioned so changes are auditable.

howSteps.title: Live-apply
howSteps.body: Pronunciation updates apply to in-flight calls within seconds. No re-deploy, no dropped calls.

benefits.title: Phonetic or audio sample
benefits.body: IPA/ARPAbet for predictable terms. Upload a real audio clip for tricky names + product SKUs.

benefits.title: Per-finn scoping
benefits.body: Sales finn says 'Acme' one way; legal finn might say it differently. No collision.

benefits.title: Versioned dictionary
benefits.body: Diff view per change. Roll back. Audit-ready for compliance + brand teams.

benefits.title: Live updates
benefits.body: Add a word, fix a mispronunciation, apply to all in-flight calls within seconds.

faqs.question: What formats can I provide?
faqs.answer: Phonetic (IPA or ARPAbet), or upload a 1-second audio sample of the correct pronunciation. Audio takes precedence when both are provided.

faqs.question: How is the dictionary scoped?
faqs.answer: Per-finn by default. Optional account-wide dictionary for shared brand terms. Per-workflow overrides supported.

faqs.question: Are pronunciation changes versioned?
faqs.answer: Yes — every dictionary edit creates a versioned snapshot. Diff view shows exactly what changed. One-click rollback.

faqs.question: Do updates apply mid-call?
faqs.answer: Yes — dictionary changes apply to in-flight calls within seconds. No re-deploy. No dropped calls.

faqs.question: How many words can the dictionary hold?
faqs.answer: Soft cap at 5,000 entries per finn (more available on enterprise). Performance is unaffected at any reasonable size.

ivr-digit-press.hero:
Press 1 for sales.
Finn presses it.
ivr-digit-press.lead: Finn navigates legacy IVR menus on outbound calls — presses digits, listens for the right branch, gets the human or system you actually need.
ivr-digit-press.closingHeading:
Stop burning reps
on phone trees.
ivr-digit-press.closingBody: Finn handles the 'press 1, press 2' navigation automatically — your reps land on the human, not the hold queue.

howSteps.title: Detect the menu
howSteps.body: ASR transcribes the IVR prompt in real time. Workflow matches against expected branches.

howSteps.title: Generate DTMF tones
howSteps.body: Sub-100ms tone generation for the right digit. Or speak the choice if the IVR accepts voice.

howSteps.title: Branch or escalate
howSteps.body: Conditional logic on what comes next — human pickup, voicemail, second menu, hold queue. All handled.

benefits.title: DTMF + voice navigation
benefits.body: Generates tones for digit-press menus. Speaks choices for voice-driven IVRs. Both work.

benefits.title: Conditional branching
benefits.body: Different paths based on what the IVR says. 'Account number?' → speak it. 'Press 1?' → press it.

benefits.title: Hold-queue tolerance
benefits.body: When the IVR drops Finn into a queue, Finn waits patiently + signals when a human picks up.

benefits.title: Every press logged
benefits.body: Audit trail of every digit + every spoken response. QA-ready transcript.

faqs.question: What's the use case for IVR navigation?
faqs.answer: Outbound calls to legacy systems — utilities, insurers, hospitals, government agencies that haven't modernized. Finn navigates their menus so your reps don't have to.

faqs.question: How does Finn know which digit to press?
faqs.answer: ASR transcribes the IVR prompt in real time. Workflow logic matches expected branches ('For sales, press 1' → 1).

faqs.question: Can Finn handle voice-driven IVRs?
faqs.answer: Yes — Finn speaks the menu choice ('Sales') in addition to DTMF tones. Both modes supported per workflow.

faqs.question: What happens when the IVR drops Finn in a hold queue?
faqs.answer: Finn waits patiently with periodic VAD checks. When a human picks up, Finn detects + signals the workflow to take the next action (escalate to your rep, deliver the message, etc.).

faqs.question: Is the navigation auditable?
faqs.answer: Yes — every digit press + spoken response logged with timestamp. Full transcript available on the call timeline.

business-hours-routing.lead: Route inbound calls by time of day + caller timezone. Business hours go to humans, after-hours to Finn, holidays to a custom message. All in one config.
business-hours-routing.closingHeading:
Finn covers nights.
Humans cover days.
business-hours-routing.closingBody: Smart business-hours routing keeps reps focused during their shift + lets Finn handle everything else — overflow, after-hours, holidays.

howSteps.title: Define your schedule
howSteps.body: Per-region business hours, holiday calendar, lunch breaks if you want. Multiple schedules per finn.

howSteps.title: Detect caller timezone
howSteps.body: Inbound caller area code + locale data → timezone. Inbound from a Mumbai number routes against IST hours.

howSteps.title: Route the call
howSteps.body: In hours → human queue. Out of hours → Finn handles it. Holiday → custom message + callback offer.

benefits.title: Multiple schedules per finn
benefits.body: Sales hours, support hours, escalation hours — different rules, same workflow.

benefits.title: Country holiday calendars
benefits.body: Built-in calendars for US, UK, EU, India, Australia, more. Manual overrides for company-specific dates.

benefits.title: Overflow handling
benefits.body: When the queue fills during business hours, Finn picks up the overflow instead of letting calls drop.

benefits.title: Live-editable
benefits.body: Update hours mid-week — applies to in-flight calls within seconds. No re-deploy.

faqs.question: How is caller timezone detected?
faqs.answer: Area code mapping for US/Canada (~95% accurate). International caller ID + country code for global. Override via CRM lookup when caller is known.

faqs.question: Can I configure different schedules per workflow?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sales workflow uses sales hours. Support workflow uses support hours. Different finns can share or split schedules.

faqs.question: How are holidays handled?
faqs.answer: Built-in country holiday calendars (US, UK, EU, India, Australia, more). Override any date for company-specific closures (e.g. company offsites).

faqs.question: What happens during overflow?
faqs.answer: When the human queue fills past your threshold, Finn picks up the overflow + handles or schedules callback. No dropped calls.

faqs.question: Can I switch routing mid-shift?
faqs.answer: Yes — manual override from the dashboard. Useful for emergencies, all-hands meetings, weather closures.

skill-based-queues.hero:
Route by skill.
Not by random.
skill-based-queues.lead: Match callers to specialists based on intent, language, account tier, geography — any signal. Skill-based queues turn round-robin into real targeting.
skill-based-queues.closingHeading:
Stop transferring twice.
Land it on the first try.
skill-based-queues.closingBody: Skill-based routing matches the caller to the right specialist on first connect. Fewer transfers, faster resolutions, happier callers + agents.

howSteps.title: Define skill tags
howSteps.body: Per-agent: languages spoken, tier authority, vertical expertise, account ownership. Per-queue: required skills.

howSteps.title: Inspect the call
howSteps.body: Caller intent, captured fields, account tier, language — surfaced before the routing decision.

howSteps.title: Pick the best match
howSteps.body: Weighted matching with fallback rules. Routes to top-skill match available; ladders down if no match free.

benefits.title: Multi-signal matching
benefits.body: Skill, tier, language, geography, account ownership — all available as routing inputs.

benefits.title: Weighted priorities
benefits.body: 'Language match > skill match > availability' — tunable per workflow.

benefits.title: Ladder fallback
benefits.body: No skill match free? Falls back to next-best with a configurable ladder. No caller stranded.

benefits.title: Live queue dashboard
benefits.body: Real-time view of queue depth, wait times, agent availability per skill. Adjustable mid-shift.

faqs.question: How are skill tags managed?
faqs.answer: Per-agent profile in the dashboard. Add/remove skills, set proficiency levels, define availability windows. Bulk-import from your existing HR or workforce-management system.

faqs.question: What signals can drive routing?
faqs.answer: Intent (from voice analysis), captured fields (account number, product), language detected, geography (caller area code), CRM-derived signals (tier, owner, history), custom workflow flags.

faqs.question: How does the fallback ladder work?
faqs.answer: Configurable per workflow. Default: prefer exact skill match → 1-level-down match → any available. Can be tightened (queue forever) or loosened (any-skill ok) per business rule.

faqs.question: Can I see queue stats in real time?
faqs.answer: Yes — dashboard shows queue depth per skill, wait times per tier, agent utilization. Filter + drill in. Adjustable thresholds + alerts.

faqs.question: What if no agent matches?
faqs.answer: Configurable: leave on hold with periodic check-in, schedule callback, route to Finn for first-pass handling + escalate later.

hold-music-alternative.lead: Replace generic hold music with branded micro-interactions — status updates, quick captures, FAQ answers — while the caller waits for a human.
hold-music-alternative.closingHeading:
Turn dead air
into live conversation.
hold-music-alternative.closingBody: Hold-music alternative keeps the caller engaged + warms them up for the human. Faster handoff, higher CSAT, no muzak fatigue.

howSteps.title: Detect hold state
howSteps.body: When a caller enters a queue, Finn picks up the conversation — status, ETA, branded intro.

howSteps.title: Capture useful info
howSteps.body: Pre-collect fields the human will need anyway — account number, reason for call, urgency. Saves the rep 30 seconds.

howSteps.title: Hand off when available
howSteps.body: Human picks up with the captured fields + Finn's summary pre-loaded. Caller doesn't repeat themselves.

benefits.title: Pre-captures the obvious
benefits.body: Account number, reason for call, urgency — collected during hold so the rep doesn't start from scratch.

benefits.title: Real-time ETA updates
benefits.body: '2 callers ahead of you' beats 'thank you for your patience' on loop.

benefits.title: Per-queue scripts
benefits.body: Different hold scripts for sales vs support vs collections. Brand voice consistent across them all.

benefits.title: Reduced abandon rate
benefits.body: Engaged callers stay on the line. Typical 25-40% drop in abandons vs muzak baseline.

faqs.question: How is this different from a normal IVR?
faqs.answer: IVRs are decision menus you press through. Hold-music alternative is a live conversation that fills wait time productively — collecting fields, surfacing status, answering quick FAQs while the queue moves.

faqs.question: Does it actually reduce abandon rates?
faqs.answer: Typical customers see 25-40% drop in queue abandons vs muzak baseline. Engaged callers stay on the line.

faqs.question: Can the captured info be pushed to the rep?
faqs.answer: Yes — handoff includes the fields Finn captured + a one-line summary, screen-popped into your CRM or helpdesk.

faqs.question: What if the queue is empty when Finn finishes hold-script?
faqs.answer: Configurable: route immediately, loop to a different script, offer callback, switch to ambient hold music as fallback.

faqs.question: Can I customize the script per queue?
faqs.answer: Yes — every queue can have its own hold workflow. Different tone for VIP queue vs general support.

byoc-sip-trunk.lead: Don't want to switch telephony providers? Wire Finn into your existing Twilio, Plivo, Telnyx, or any SIP trunk. Same observability, same workflow.
byoc-sip-trunk.closingHeading:
Keep your numbers.
Keep your rates.
byoc-sip-trunk.closingBody: Bring-your-own-carrier (BYOC) gets you Finn's intelligence on top of telephony you already trust. No number porting, no contract change.

howSteps.title: Connect your provider
howSteps.body: Twilio, Plivo, Telnyx — paste credentials, point your numbers at Finn's SIP endpoint. ~10 minutes setup.

howSteps.title: Map numbers to workflows
howSteps.body: Per-number routing to a Finn workflow. Mix BYOC numbers + Finn-provided numbers in the same account.

howSteps.title: Same observability
howSteps.body: Dashboard treats BYOC + Finn numbers identically. Same call logs, transcripts, PCA, costs, audits.

benefits.title: Major providers first-class
benefits.body: Twilio, Plivo, Telnyx have one-click setup + full feature parity (DTMF, SMS, MMS, transfers).

benefits.title: Generic SIP trunk fallback
benefits.body: Any SIP-compatible provider works. Your enterprise PBX, your in-house Asterisk, your weird legacy stack — all supported.

benefits.title: Per-number routing
benefits.body: Mix BYOC and Finn-provided numbers in one account. Route each number to a different workflow.

benefits.title: Unified billing observability
benefits.body: Finn platform fees billed by us. Carrier minutes billed by your carrier. Cost dashboard shows both.

faqs.question: Which carriers does BYOC support?
faqs.answer: Twilio, Plivo, Telnyx as first-class (one-click setup, full feature parity). Any SIP-compatible carrier via generic SIP trunk integration.

faqs.question: Do I need to port my numbers?
faqs.answer: No. Your numbers stay with your carrier. We just point your SIP routing at Finn's endpoint for inbound calls + handle outbound through your carrier's API.

faqs.question: Are SMS / MMS supported under BYOC?
faqs.answer: Yes — first-class for Twilio, Plivo, Telnyx. Generic SIP carriers depend on what they support; usually voice-only.

faqs.question: How is billing split?
faqs.answer: Finn platform fees billed by us. Carrier voice minutes + SMS billed directly by your carrier on your existing contract. No double-charging.

faqs.question: Can I mix BYOC + Finn-provided numbers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Common pattern: BYOC for established main numbers (preserve reputation + branded ID); Finn-provided for new outbound campaigns + experimental flows.

multi-line-concurrency.lead: Unlimited concurrent calls per Finn — capped only by your plan. No queues, no busy signals, no 'all our agents are helping other customers.'
multi-line-concurrency.closingBody: Finn scales to whatever you throw at it. Spike day, holiday rush, ad-driven surge — same response time, same quality, no queue.

howSteps.title: Account-level concurrency cap
howSteps.body: Set or buy concurrency per plan. Cap shared across all finns OR partitioned per finn — your call.

howSteps.title: Live throttle controls
howSteps.body: Mid-campaign throttle — limit outbound concurrency to keep your downstream CRM, support team, or compliance window stable.

howSteps.title: Per-second billing
howSteps.body: Pay only for active call seconds. Idle concurrency capacity = free. Spike days don't bankrupt you.

benefits.title: Unlimited per-finn concurrency
benefits.body: One finn can answer 10 calls or 10,000 simultaneously. No per-agent provisioning.

benefits.title: Live throttle
benefits.body: Cap outbound concurrency to protect downstream systems. Adjustable mid-campaign.

benefits.title: Per-second billing
benefits.body: Idle capacity = free. Only active call seconds billed. Spike days stay affordable.

benefits.title: Cross-region capacity
benefits.body: Capacity allocated dynamically across regions for lowest-latency routing.

faqs.question: Is there a hard cap on concurrent calls?
faqs.answer: Per-plan caps that scale on demand. Most starter accounts come with 25 concurrent; enterprise customers run thousands. Burstable on request.

faqs.question: How is concurrency billed?
faqs.answer: Per-second active call time. Idle concurrency capacity is free — you pay only when calls are actually live.

faqs.question: Can I cap concurrency per finn?
faqs.answer: Yes — partition account-level capacity across finns, or share a single pool. Useful for protecting downstream systems (CRM rate limits, support team capacity).

faqs.question: What happens when the cap is hit?
faqs.answer: Configurable per workflow: queue + retry, drop with callback offer, divert to voicemail, throttle outbound rate. No silent dropped calls.

faqs.question: How does Finn handle regional latency at scale?
faqs.answer: Capacity allocated dynamically across regions so calls route to the lowest-latency edge. Sub-second voice loop maintained globally.

calendar-sync.hero:
Books on the calendar
callers actually use.
calendar-sync.lead: Two-way sync with Google Calendar, Outlook, Office 365. Read live availability, write confirmed bookings, fire invite emails — all mid-call.
calendar-sync.closingBody: Calendar sync prevents double-bookings + ensures the caller leaves with a confirmed slot, an invite email, and the right rep blocked out.

howSteps.title: Connect Google / Outlook
howSteps.body: OAuth flow — pick which calendars to sync. Per-rep or per-resource calendars supported.

howSteps.title: Read availability mid-call
howSteps.body: Finn queries free/busy in real time. Offers 2-3 slots that match caller constraints + rep availability.

howSteps.title: Write the booking
howSteps.body: Caller picks a slot. Finn books it, fires the invite email, blocks the rep's calendar — all before hang-up.

benefits.title: Native Google + Outlook + O365
benefits.body: First-class OAuth integration. No middleware, no Zapier glue, no stale availability.

benefits.title: Round-robin or specific rep
benefits.body: Book the next available rep, or pin to a specific named rep if the caller asked.

benefits.title: Buffer + drive-time aware
benefits.body: Pre-call buffers, post-call buffers, drive-time between in-person appointments — all configurable per workflow.

benefits.title: Auto-invite + reminder emails
benefits.body: ICS invite fires on book. Reminder emails + SMS scheduled automatically.

faqs.question: Which calendar systems are supported?
faqs.answer: Google Calendar, Outlook, Office 365, iCloud Calendar, plus Cal.com + Calendly as scheduling-layer integrations. Two-way sync (read + write) on all.

faqs.question: Can Finn book on specific reps' calendars?
faqs.answer: Yes — round-robin across a pool, or pinned to a specific rep when caller asked for them. Per-rep availability windows + skill matching supported.

faqs.question: How fast is the slot check?
faqs.answer: Sub-second — Finn queries free/busy from the calendar API in real time. No stale 'best guesses.'

faqs.question: What about buffers and drive time?
faqs.answer: Pre-call + post-call buffers, drive-time between in-person appointments, and per-rep custom rules all configurable per workflow.

faqs.question: Does the booking fire an invite email?
faqs.answer: Yes — ICS invite + reminder emails + optional SMS reminders all fire automatically on booking. Per-workflow templates.

calcom-integration.lead: If your team already lives in Cal.com or Calendly, Finn books through your existing event types. Same links, same rules, same logs.
calcom-integration.closingHeading:
Don't rebuild scheduling.
Wire it in.
calcom-integration.closingBody: Cal.com + Calendly integration preserves your existing scheduling rules, branding, and reporting. Finn just hands the slot via voice.

howSteps.title: Connect via API key
howSteps.body: Paste Cal.com or Calendly API key. We pull your event types automatically.

howSteps.title: Map event types to workflows
howSteps.body: Per-workflow: 'discovery call' → 30-min event type. 'demo' → 45-min event type. Different finns can use different event types.

howSteps.title: Book via voice
howSteps.body: Caller picks a slot, Finn books through Cal.com/Calendly API. Existing booking rules (buffers, host assignment, reminders) all honored.

benefits.title: Event-type aware
benefits.body: Round-robin, group, collective, fixed-host — all event types supported. Finn picks the right slot per event-type rules.

benefits.title: Existing branding preserved
benefits.body: Confirmation emails + reschedule pages stay in your Cal.com/Calendly branding. No surprise system in the inbox.

benefits.title: Bookings appear in your dashboard
benefits.body: Finn-booked appointments show in Cal.com/Calendly admin. Same reports, same workflows downstream.

benefits.title: Reschedule + cancel via voice
benefits.body: Caller calls back to reschedule? Finn looks up the existing booking + handles the change through Cal.com API.

faqs.question: Why use Cal.com or Calendly instead of native calendar sync?
faqs.answer: If your team already lives in one of these tools, the booking experience (event types, branding, reporting, reminders) is preserved end-to-end. Finn just adds voice as a booking channel.

faqs.question: Which event types work?
faqs.answer: All major Cal.com + Calendly types — round-robin, group, collective, fixed-host. Finn picks the right slot per the event-type's existing rules.

faqs.question: Are bookings tracked in my Cal.com/Calendly admin?
faqs.answer: Yes — Finn-booked appointments appear in your admin dashboard like any other booking. Same reports, same workflows downstream.

faqs.question: Can Finn handle reschedules?
faqs.answer: Yes — if the caller calls back to reschedule, Finn looks up the existing booking + handles change through the platform API.

faqs.question: What about cancellations?
faqs.answer: Same flow — voice cancellation hits the Cal.com/Calendly API + fires whatever cancel-flow you have configured (rep notification, refund, etc.).

conditional-slot-logic.hero:
Right slot.
Right rep. Every time.
conditional-slot-logic.lead: Conditional booking logic — match the caller to the right calendar based on intent, geography, account tier, product, or any captured field.
conditional-slot-logic.closingHeading:
Stop sending
everyone to the same calendar.
conditional-slot-logic.closingBody: Conditional slot logic books the right caller with the right rep on the first try. Higher conversion, fewer rescheduled handoffs.

howSteps.title: Capture decision signals
howSteps.body: Intent, geography, account tier, product interest, language — captured fields drive the routing decision.

howSteps.title: Branch to the right calendar
howSteps.body: Enterprise lead → senior rep calendar. SMB lead → SDR calendar. Spanish speaker → Spanish-speaking rep.

howSteps.title: Book with fallback ladder
howSteps.body: No exact match available in the window? Falls back to next-best calendar per configurable ladder.

benefits.title: Multi-signal routing
benefits.body: Match on intent, tier, geography, product, language — any captured field.

benefits.title: Per-workflow rules
benefits.body: Sales has one rule set. Support has another. Different finns, different logic.

benefits.title: Fallback ladders
benefits.body: No exact match? Ladder gracefully down to next-best. Never strands a caller without a slot.

benefits.title: Live A/B test
benefits.body: Test 'route by tier' vs 'route by intent' — measure conversion per branch in PCA.

faqs.question: What signals can drive booking decisions?
faqs.answer: Intent (from voice analysis), geography (area code or stated location), account tier (CRM lookup), product interest (captured field), language detected, custom workflow flags.

faqs.question: How is fallback handled?
faqs.answer: Configurable per workflow. Default: prefer exact match → next-best tier → any available with 24h. Can be tightened (no fallback, callback offer) or loosened per business rule.

faqs.question: Can I A/B test routing rules?
faqs.answer: Yes — split traffic across branches + measure conversion + show-rate in PCA. Useful for tuning round-robin pools or testing new routing signals.

faqs.question: Are rules editable mid-campaign?
faqs.answer: Yes — live edit from the dashboard. Changes apply to in-flight calls within seconds. No re-deploy.

faqs.question: Can I see which rule fired for a given booking?
faqs.answer: Yes — every booking logs the decision path (which signals were captured, which branch fired, what the fallback was). Audit-ready.

reschedule-on-noshow.lead: When a caller misses an appointment, Finn calls back within minutes, acknowledges the miss, offers a new slot — and books it.
reschedule-on-noshow.closingHeading:
Don't lose
the lead to a no-show.
reschedule-on-noshow.closingBody: No-show recovery runs automatically. Most rebooked within the same day. Conversion lift typically 12-20% on missed-appointment cohorts.

howSteps.title: Detect the miss
howSteps.body: Calendar status check 10 min after slot start — marked no-show? Triggers the workflow.

howSteps.title: Friendly callback
howSteps.body: Finn calls within 5 minutes. Acknowledges the miss without guilt-tripping. Offers new slots.

howSteps.title: Reslot or escalate
howSteps.body: Books a new slot if caller picks one. If declined or unreachable, queues a follow-up callback or escalates to a rep.

benefits.title: 5-minute callback
benefits.body: Strike while the caller's intent is still warm. Same-day rebook rates ~60% in steady state.

benefits.title: Templated, non-guilty tone
benefits.body: 'Looks like we missed each other — happens. Want to try again?' Not 'You missed your appointment.'

benefits.title: Configurable retry ladder
benefits.body: 1st attempt 5min after miss. 2nd attempt 2hrs. 3rd attempt next day. All tunable per workflow.

benefits.title: Escalation rules
benefits.body: After N misses, escalate to a human rep, mark lead cold, or trigger nurture drip — your choice.

faqs.question: How is a no-show detected?
faqs.answer: Calendar status check 10 minutes after slot start time. If marked 'no-show' (manually or via CRM integration), the recovery workflow fires.

faqs.question: What's the typical recovery rate?
faqs.answer: 60% same-day rebook rate in steady state. Industries with high time-sensitivity (healthcare appointments, mortgage call-backs) see higher; loose-funnel sales see lower.

faqs.question: How quickly does Finn call back?
faqs.answer: 5 minutes after detected miss by default. Tunable from 1 minute (aggressive) to several hours (passive recovery).

faqs.question: Does this work for in-person appointments?
faqs.answer: Yes — works equally well for in-person, video, or phone appointments. Recovery flow is channel-agnostic.

faqs.question: What if the caller never picks up the recovery call?
faqs.answer: Configurable retry ladder (default 5min → 2hr → next day). After N misses, escalate to human, drop to nurture drip, or mark cold per workflow.

appointment-reminders.lead: Automated appointment reminders via voice, SMS, WhatsApp, or email — 24h before, 1h before, configurable. Caller can confirm, reschedule, or cancel inline.
appointment-reminders.closingHeading:
Reminders that
actually get a response.
appointment-reminders.closingBody: Multi-channel appointment reminders with two-way response handling. Typical 30-50% reduction in no-show rates within 30 days.

howSteps.title: Configure the cadence
howSteps.body: 24h reminder, 1h reminder, day-of voice call — pick the combination per workflow. Templated body + channel.

howSteps.title: Send via right channel
howSteps.body: SMS for quick confirms. Voice for high-value or high-risk appointments. WhatsApp where it's the dominant channel.

howSteps.title: Handle responses two-way
howSteps.body: 'Confirmed' → mark + skip subsequent reminders. 'Reschedule' → trigger booking flow. 'Cancel' → free the slot + offer to rebook later.

benefits.title: Voice + SMS + WhatsApp + email
benefits.body: Different appointment types want different channels. Configurable per workflow.

benefits.title: Two-way response
benefits.body: 'STOP' opts out. 'RESCHEDULE' fires the booking flow. 'CONFIRMED' marks + suppresses follow-ups.

benefits.title: Show-up rate lift
benefits.body: Typical 30-50% reduction in no-show rates within 30 days.

benefits.title: Cadence templates
benefits.body: Healthcare default, sales-demo default, hospitality default — pre-built cadences for common verticals.

faqs.question: Which channels are supported for reminders?
faqs.answer: Voice calls, SMS, WhatsApp, email — pick one or any combination per workflow. Different appointment types can use different channels.

faqs.question: What's the typical no-show reduction?
faqs.answer: 30-50% reduction within 30 days for most customers. Higher for healthcare + high-value B2B appointments; lower for low-friction consumer bookings.

faqs.question: Can callers confirm or reschedule via the reminder?
faqs.answer: Yes — two-way handling. SMS 'CONFIRMED' marks + suppresses. 'RESCHEDULE' fires the booking workflow. Voice reminders accept verbal confirm/reschedule/cancel.

faqs.question: Are there pre-built cadences for common industries?
faqs.answer: Yes — healthcare, sales-demo, hospitality, consumer-service templates. Customize body + timing per workflow.

faqs.question: How does cadence interact with calendar updates?
faqs.answer: If the appointment moves (rep reschedules from their side), reminder cadence auto-recalculates from the new slot time.

audience-csv-upload.hero:
Upload a CSV.
Dial in minutes.
audience-csv-upload.lead: Drop a CSV, map columns to workflow variables, validate phones + DNC, hit go. From spreadsheet to live outbound campaign in under five minutes.
audience-csv-upload.closingBody: Audience upload handles the messy part — column mapping, phone validation, DNC scrub, timezone inference — so you go from CSV to launched campaign without IT involvement.

howSteps.title: Drop the file
howSteps.body: CSV, XLSX, or push via API. Up to 1M rows per upload. Sub-30s import.

howSteps.title: Auto-map columns
howSteps.body: Phone, name, custom fields auto-detected + mapped to workflow variables. Manual override if needed.

howSteps.title: Validate + launch
howSteps.body: Phone format check, DNC scrub, timezone inference, opt-out cross-check. Preview 10 sample calls. Hit go.

benefits.title: Auto column mapping
benefits.body: Phone, name, email, custom fields detected by header name + content. Override when needed.

benefits.title: Built-in validation
benefits.body: E.164 phone format check, DNC scrub, opt-out cross-reference, timezone inference — all on import.

benefits.title: Sample preview
