benefits.body: Preview 10 sample calls with merged variables before launching. Catch template bugs before they go out.

benefits.title: CRM auto-sync
benefits.body: Schedule recurring imports from Salesforce, HubSpot, or any CRM. New leads dialed automatically.

faqs.question: What file formats are supported?
faqs.answer: CSV (recommended), XLSX, JSON. Up to 1M rows per upload. Larger volumes via API push.

faqs.question: How does column mapping work?
faqs.answer: Auto-detection by header name + content sniffing (phone numbers, emails, names). Manual override per column. Mappings remembered per workflow for repeat uploads.

faqs.question: Is DNC scrubbing automatic?
faqs.answer: Yes — national + state DNC lists checked on import. Per-account opt-out lists cross-referenced. Bad numbers flagged + excluded before dial.

faqs.question: Can I sync from my CRM instead of uploading?
faqs.answer: Yes — first-class Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho. Generic webhook + REST for any other system. Schedule recurring syncs (hourly, daily) or push on-demand.

faqs.question: What about row-level errors?
faqs.answer: Each row validated independently. Bad rows surfaced in a per-import report — fix + re-import the failed subset, valid rows dial without blocking.

ab-test-workflows.lead: Split traffic between two workflow versions, measure conversion + sentiment per branch, ship the winner. Statistical significance built in.
ab-test-workflows.closingBody: A/B test workflows ship variants in parallel + measure what actually works. No more 'I think this prompt is better.'

howSteps.title: Create the variant
howSteps.body: Clone the live workflow, change what you want to test — prompt wording, voice, branching, sequence. Versioned snapshot.

howSteps.title: Split traffic
howSteps.body: Configurable % split (50/50, 90/10 for risky changes). Per-call random assignment, no caller sees both.

howSteps.title: Track + decide
howSteps.body: Live dashboard shows conversion, CSAT, call duration, abandon rate per branch. Stat sig auto-detected. Ship winner with one click.

benefits.title: Multi-metric tracking
benefits.body: Conversion, sentiment, call duration, abandon rate, escalation rate — all measured per branch automatically.

benefits.title: Stat sig detection
benefits.body: Built-in chi-square / t-test stat sig. Surface 'variant B wins (95% confidence)' when enough data lands.

benefits.title: Per-call random assignment
benefits.body: Each caller gets one branch consistently. No caller experiences both during the test.

benefits.title: One-click promotion
benefits.body: Pick the winner from the dashboard. Auto-promotes variant to 100% traffic + retires the loser.

faqs.question: What can I A/B test?
faqs.answer: Any workflow change — prompt wording, voice persona, branching logic, capture order, escalation rules. Even entire workflows if they handle the same intent.

faqs.question: How is stat sig calculated?
faqs.answer: Built-in chi-square (for binary metrics like conversion) + t-test (for continuous metrics like call duration). Surfaces 'variant B wins at 95% confidence' when enough data lands.

faqs.question: Does the same caller ever get both variants?
faqs.answer: No — per-call random assignment is sticky to the contact ID. Repeat callers get the same branch consistently across calls.

faqs.question: How long do tests typically run?
faqs.answer: Depends on volume + effect size. Small-effect tests on low-volume workflows = weeks. Big-effect tests on high-volume campaigns = hours. Dashboard estimates time-to-significance live.

faqs.question: Can I run more than 2 variants?
faqs.answer: Yes — N-way splits (A/B/C/D) supported. Stat sig math accounts for multiple-comparison correction automatically.

evals-scoring.hero:
Score every call.
Against your own rubric.
evals-scoring.lead: Define quality criteria — script adherence, tone, compliance phrases, captured fields — and Finn scores every call automatically. No human QA sampling.
evals-scoring.closingBody: Evals scoring runs against every call, surfaces drift the moment it happens, and gives you a per-workflow quality dashboard that updates in real time.

howSteps.title: Build the rubric
howSteps.body: Per-workflow criteria — required phrases, prohibited phrases, tone, compliance disclosures, capture completeness. LLM-judged + rule-based mixed.

howSteps.title: Auto-score every call
howSteps.body: PCA pipeline runs scoring at call end. Sub-30s from call disconnect to full score breakdown.

howSteps.title: Track drift live
howSteps.body: Dashboard shows score trends per workflow. Alerts on drops below threshold. Compare across cohorts, time windows, rep assignments.

benefits.title: Custom rubrics per workflow
benefits.body: Sales workflow scores differently than collections. Build the criteria that match how your team defines quality.

benefits.title: LLM-judged + rule-based
benefits.body: Subjective criteria (tone, empathy) judged by LLM. Objective criteria (required phrases) matched by rules. Best of both.

benefits.title: 100% call coverage
benefits.body: Every call scored. Not a 5% sample. Drift surfaces immediately, not weeks later.

benefits.title: Compliance audit trail
benefits.body: Required-phrase compliance auto-tracked + reportable. Useful for TCPA, GDPR, HIPAA disclosures.

faqs.question: What can I score against?
faqs.answer: Anything observable in the transcript or call metadata — required phrases, prohibited words, tone, sentiment, capture completeness, workflow path taken, duration, escalation outcome.

faqs.question: How are subjective criteria like 'empathy' scored?
faqs.answer: LLM judges with calibrated prompts. Each workflow's rubric can include subjective criteria that are scored against examples + rubric language you define.

faqs.question: How accurate is automated scoring?
faqs.answer: Calibrated against human QA samples — typically 85-92% agreement with human scorers in the steady state. Outliers surfaced for human review.

faqs.question: Can I see trending over time?
faqs.answer: Yes — per-workflow score trends, per-rep, per-cohort, per-time-window. Set alert thresholds for drift detection.

faqs.question: Is the rubric versioned?
faqs.answer: Yes — rubric changes are versioned. Past calls scored against the rubric that was active when they happened. Re-score on demand if rubric changes.

per-finn-redaction.hero:
PII out
before it goes anywhere.
per-finn-redaction.lead: Per-finn redaction rules strip credit cards, SSNs, account numbers, PHI from transcripts + logs the moment they're captured. Compliance-ready by default.
per-finn-redaction.closingHeading:
Don't leak
what you don't need.
per-finn-redaction.closingBody: Per-finn redaction keeps PII out of transcripts, logs, analytics, and exports. HIPAA, PCI, GDPR, DPDP — handled the right way.

howSteps.title: Define what to redact
howSteps.body: Per-finn rules: credit card, SSN, account number, medical record, custom regex. Built-in templates for HIPAA / PCI / GDPR baselines.

howSteps.title: Auto-detect + redact
howSteps.body: Pattern detection runs on the live transcript + captured fields. Matches replaced with token (`[CC]`, `[SSN]`) before storage.

howSteps.title: Role-based reveal
howSteps.body: Authorized roles can reveal redacted values via audit-logged request. Everyone else sees only the token. Full audit trail.

benefits.title: Pre-storage redaction
benefits.body: PII never lands in transcripts, logs, analytics, or exports. Reduces breach blast radius + audit scope.

benefits.title: Custom regex rules
benefits.body: Industry-specific patterns — medical record numbers, account IDs, insurance member IDs — defined per finn.

benefits.title: Role-based reveal
benefits.body: Compliance officer can reveal a redacted SSN with audit trail. Analyst sees only token. Engineer sees only token.

benefits.title: Templates for common regimes
benefits.body: HIPAA baseline, PCI baseline, GDPR baseline — pre-built rules to flip on per finn.

faqs.question: What PII can Finn auto-detect?
faqs.answer: Credit card numbers (Luhn-validated), SSN, US/EU phone numbers, email addresses, IBANs, medical record numbers, custom regex patterns. Templates for HIPAA / PCI / GDPR baselines.

faqs.question: Where does redaction happen?
faqs.answer: Before storage. The unredacted value never lands in your transcripts, logs, analytics, or exports — only the redaction token (`[CC]`, `[SSN]`).

faqs.question: Can authorized users see the original value?
faqs.answer: Yes — role-based reveal. Compliance officers can reveal via audit-logged request. Audit trail captures who revealed what, when.

faqs.question: Does redaction affect the live call?
faqs.answer: No — Finn still uses the unredacted value during the call (e.g. to verify a credit card with your payment processor). Redaction is post-call, pre-storage.

faqs.question: Is the redaction regime configurable per finn?
faqs.answer: Yes — each finn has its own rules. HIPAA-restricted health finn uses one set; sales finn uses a lighter set. No cross-contamination.

data-residency-picker.hero:
Pick the region.
Lock it in.
data-residency-picker.lead: US, EU, India, Australia, Canada — pick the region your data lives in. Call recordings, transcripts, analytics all stay within the chosen jurisdiction.
data-residency-picker.closingHeading:
Data stays
where you put it.
data-residency-picker.closingBody: Data residency picker locks your storage region at provisioning. Recordings, transcripts, analytics, and backups all stay within jurisdiction. Audit + attestation ready.

howSteps.title: Pick at provisioning
howSteps.body: US, EU, India, Australia, Canada. Choose at account creation; lock prevents accidental cross-region writes downstream.

howSteps.title: All data stays in-region
howSteps.body: Recordings, transcripts, captured fields, PCA outputs, backups — all in-region. Cross-region access requires explicit cross-border safeguards.

howSteps.title: Audit + attestation
howSteps.body: Per-account residency attestation document available on demand. SOC 2 + ISO 27001 audits cover regional separation.

benefits.title: Per-account region lock
benefits.body: Region chosen at provisioning + locked. Prevents accidental cross-region writes via API or admin error.

benefits.title: Full data scope
benefits.body: Recordings, transcripts, captured fields, PCA, backups, analytics — all in-region. No 'metadata exception.'

benefits.title: Cross-border safeguards
benefits.body: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), EU-US Data Privacy Framework (DPF), India DPDP-compliant. Auditable cross-border transfer logs.

benefits.title: Residency attestation
benefits.body: On-demand attestation document for your compliance team. Useful for vendor reviews + regulatory filings.

faqs.question: Which regions are available?
faqs.answer: US (us-east-1 + us-west-2), EU (eu-west-1 in Ireland + eu-central-1 in Frankfurt), India (ap-south-1 in Mumbai), Australia (ap-southeast-2 in Sydney), Canada (ca-central-1).

faqs.question: Can I change region after provisioning?
faqs.answer: Region is locked after account creation to prevent accidental cross-region writes. Migration to a different region is a manual, audit-logged operation handled by our compliance team on request.

faqs.question: What data is covered by residency?
faqs.answer: All of it — call recordings, transcripts, captured fields, PCA outputs, analytics, backups. No 'metadata exception.'

faqs.question: How are cross-border accesses handled?
faqs.answer: When in-region engineers can't resolve an issue, cross-region access is requested with audit-logged justification + customer notification. SCCs + DPF + DPDP safeguards in place for any transfers.

faqs.question: Can I get a residency attestation document?
faqs.answer: Yes — on-demand from your account settings. PDF + signed copy for compliance + vendor reviews. SOC 2 + ISO 27001 audits cover regional separation independently.

sentiment-timeline.lead: See the exact moment a call went sideways. Per-turn sentiment scoring surfaces the trigger word, the dropped tone, the recovery — coaching-ready.
sentiment-timeline.closingHeading:
Stop reading summaries.
Watch the inflection.
sentiment-timeline.closingBody: Sentiment timeline shows where calls go right + wrong. Coach reps on what worked. Drop prompts that didn't.

howSteps.title: Score per turn
howSteps.body: Each caller + agent turn scored on emotion + valence. Sub-30s from call end to full timeline.

howSteps.title: Visual timeline
howSteps.body: Call transcript overlaid with sentiment curve. Inflection points highlighted with the trigger turn.

howSteps.title: Filter + cohort
howSteps.body: Search calls by sentiment shift ('calls that started positive + ended negative'). Build cohorts. Compare across reps + workflows.

sentiment-timeline.benefitsHeading:
See the moment.
Fix the prompt.

benefits.title: Per-turn granularity
benefits.body: Not a single call-level score — every turn scored so you see what specifically moved the needle.

benefits.title: Multi-axis emotion
benefits.body: Not just positive / negative — emotion families (anger, confusion, satisfaction, urgency) tracked independently.

benefits.title: Inflection detection
benefits.body: Auto-surfaces the turn where sentiment shifted. Coaching surface for reps + prompt-tuning surface for workflow owners.

benefits.title: Searchable + cohortable
benefits.body: 'Show me calls that went negative in the first 30 seconds.' Build cohorts. Compare across reps, workflows, regions.

faqs.question: How granular is the sentiment scoring?
faqs.answer: Per-turn — each caller + agent utterance gets an independent sentiment score on multiple axes (valence + emotion family).

faqs.question: What signals drive sentiment scoring?
faqs.answer: Acoustic (pitch, pace, volume), lexical (word choice), conversational (turn-taking dynamics). Multi-modal model tuned on call-center data.

faqs.question: Can I search for specific sentiment patterns?
faqs.answer: Yes — search filters like 'started positive, ended negative' or 'sentiment dropped after escalation request.' Build cohorts from these queries.

faqs.question: Is sentiment scored in real time during the call?
faqs.answer: Yes — available via webhook during the call for real-time escalation triggers ('caller anger detected → escalate to human').

faqs.question: How does this differ from post-call analysis?
faqs.answer: PCA tags outcomes + structured fields per call. Sentiment timeline is the moment-to-moment emotional curve within each call. Complementary.

conversation-tagging.hero:
Tag every call.
Find every pattern.
conversation-tagging.lead: Custom tags applied automatically per call — by intent, outcome, dispute type, product mentioned. Searchable, filterable, exportable.
conversation-tagging.closingBody: Conversation tagging makes call data queryable. 'Show me all calls where caller mentioned competitor X.' 'Filter to calls flagged as billing disputes.'

howSteps.title: Define your taxonomy
howSteps.body: Per-workflow tags — intent types, dispute categories, competitor mentions, escalation triggers. Mix LLM-judged + rule-based.

howSteps.title: Auto-tag on call end
howSteps.body: PCA pipeline applies tags within 30s of call end. Multi-tag (not mutually exclusive) by default.

howSteps.title: Search + export
howSteps.body: Filter calls by any tag combination. Bulk export to CSV, push to webhook, build cohorts for retraining workflows.

benefits.title: Custom tag taxonomy
benefits.body: Per-workflow tags. Sales tags differently than support. Different finns, different schemas.

benefits.title: Multi-tag, not exclusive
benefits.body: A call can be 'billing dispute' + 'tier 1 escalation' + 'competitor mention.' Reality is multi-faceted.

benefits.title: LLM + rule-based mix
benefits.body: Subjective tags (caller frustration) judged by LLM. Objective tags (mentioned 'cancel') matched by rules.

benefits.title: Bulk operations
benefits.body: Find tagged calls in bulk — export CSV, push to webhook, retrain on subset, A/B test against alternates.

faqs.question: What kinds of tags can I define?
faqs.answer: Anything observable in the transcript — intent types, dispute categories, competitor mentions, escalation triggers, product references, sentiment shifts, captured field values.

faqs.question: How accurate is automated tagging?
faqs.answer: LLM-judged tags: 85-92% agreement with human taggers in steady state. Rule-based tags: 100% deterministic. Outliers surfaced for human review.

faqs.question: Can a call have multiple tags?
faqs.answer: Yes — multi-tag by default. A call can be 'billing dispute' + 'tier 1 escalation' + 'recovered' simultaneously.

faqs.question: Are tags retroactive?
faqs.answer: New tags apply forward by default. Retroactive backfill against historical calls available on demand.

faqs.question: Where can tagged calls be exported?
faqs.answer: Bulk CSV download, webhook push on tag application, direct push to your CRM or data warehouse, SQL access on enterprise.

funnel-analytics.lead: See where callers drop off. Visualize the workflow as a funnel — every node, every branch, every captured field. Pinpoint the leak.
funnel-analytics.closingBody: Funnel analytics turns every workflow into a Sankey diagram. Drop-off points obvious. Fix priorities clear.

howSteps.title: Auto-built funnel
howSteps.body: Workflow editor surfaces double as analytics nodes. Funnel auto-built — no instrumentation needed.

howSteps.title: Drop-off per node
howSteps.body: Each node shows entry / completion / drop-off counts + rates. Branch splits surface conditional path conversion.

howSteps.title: Cohort + time-series
howSteps.body: Filter by tag, region, tier, time window. Compare funnels across cohorts (campaign A vs B, region X vs Y, tier 1 vs 2).

benefits.title: Auto-instrumented
benefits.body: Funnel built automatically from the workflow. No 'track event' calls. Add a node, get the funnel.

benefits.title: Branch-aware
benefits.body: Conditional branches surface as Sankey-style splits. See which paths win + which lose.

benefits.title: Cohort comparison
benefits.body: Compare campaign A vs B, region X vs Y, tier 1 vs 2 — same funnel, different cohorts side-by-side.

benefits.title: Time-series drift
benefits.body: Funnel snapshots over time. Spot when a conversion drop started + correlate with workflow changes.

faqs.question: Do I need to instrument anything?
faqs.answer: No — funnel is auto-built from the workflow editor. Every node is automatically a measurable step. Add a node, the funnel updates.

faqs.question: How are conditional branches handled?
faqs.answer: Surfaced as Sankey-style splits. See conversion + drop-off per branch independently. Useful for tuning conditional routing.

faqs.question: Can I compare funnels across cohorts?
faqs.answer: Yes — side-by-side comparison of campaign A vs B, region X vs Y, tier 1 vs 2, time window 1 vs 2. Same funnel, different cohorts.

faqs.question: Is there time-series tracking?
faqs.answer: Yes — funnel snapshots over rolling windows. Spot when a conversion drop started + correlate with workflow versions, prompt changes, campaign launches.

faqs.question: Can I export funnel data?
faqs.answer: Yes — CSV download per filter view, plus SQL access on enterprise. Funnel-level webhooks fire on significant drift detection.

cost-per-outcome.hero:
Cost per outcome.
Not per minute.
cost-per-outcome.lead: Stop measuring 'cost per call.' Start measuring 'cost per qualified lead,' 'cost per booked demo,' 'cost per recovered payment.' That's the metric that matters.
cost-per-outcome.closingBody: Cost per outcome surfaces what each successful outcome actually costs — across workflows, campaigns, regions. CFO-ready ROI tracking.

howSteps.title: Define outcome
howSteps.body: Per-workflow outcome tags (qualified lead, booked demo, payment recovered). PCA pipeline tags every call against these.

howSteps.title: Auto-calculate cost per outcome
howSteps.body: Total spend (Finn fees + carrier minutes) / count of successful outcomes. Updated live as calls complete.

howSteps.title: Cohort + compare
howSteps.body: Filter by region, campaign, rep, time window. Compare CPO across cohorts. Spot which segments win + which leak.

cost-per-outcome.benefitsHeading:
The metric
your CFO asks about.

benefits.title: Custom outcome denominators
benefits.body: Sales workflow: cost per qualified lead. Support workflow: cost per resolved ticket. Defined per workflow.

benefits.title: All-in cost included
benefits.body: Finn platform fees + carrier minutes + SMS + email — everything attributable to a successful outcome.

benefits.title: Cohort comparison
benefits.body: Compare CPO across regions, campaigns, reps, time windows. Find what's working + what's leaking.

benefits.title: Push to your BI
benefits.body: Webhook + CSV exports for Looker, Tableau, Snowflake, Mode. CPO available wherever your team already analyzes.

faqs.question: How is 'outcome' defined?
faqs.answer: Per-workflow outcome tags (qualified lead, booked demo, payment recovered, ticket resolved). PCA pipeline tags every call against these — you pick the rubric.

faqs.question: What costs are included?
faqs.answer: Finn platform fees + carrier minutes + SMS + email send fees + any usage-based add-ons. All-in cost attributable to the workflow.

faqs.question: Can I compare CPO across cohorts?
faqs.answer: Yes — region, campaign, rep, time window, tag combinations. Side-by-side comparison + trend over time.

faqs.question: Can CPO data feed my BI tool?
faqs.answer: Yes — webhook on outcome events + CSV exports. Native connectors for Looker, Tableau, Snowflake, Mode in development.

faqs.question: What about long-tail outcomes (e.g. converted 30 days later)?
faqs.answer: Webhook-based attribution lets you mark conversions retroactively. CPO recalculates with the new denominator. Useful for delayed funnels.

audit-log-export.lead: User actions, admin changes, workflow deploys, data accesses, role grants — all logged with timestamp + actor. Bulk export for SIEM, compliance, audit.
audit-log-export.closingHeading:
Audit trail
your SOC team will love.
audit-log-export.closingBody: Audit log export streams every action to your SIEM, archives to long-term storage, supports point-in-time queries for compliance investigations.

howSteps.title: 100% action coverage
howSteps.body: Every user action, admin change, workflow deploy, data access logged with timestamp + actor + diff.

howSteps.title: Stream to SIEM
howSteps.body: Real-time push to Splunk, Datadog, Sumo, Elastic. JSON + CEF formats supported.

howSteps.title: Long-term retention
howSteps.body: Default 7-year retention. Configurable per compliance regime (HIPAA 6yr, SOX 7yr, GDPR variable). Immutable archive.

benefits.title: 100% action coverage
benefits.body: User actions, admin changes, workflow deploys, data accesses, role grants — all logged. No exceptions.

benefits.title: Real-time SIEM streaming
benefits.body: Push to Splunk, Datadog, Sumo, Elastic in real time. JSON + CEF formats supported.

benefits.title: Point-in-time queries
benefits.body: Reconstruct exact system state at any past timestamp. Useful for breach investigations + compliance audits.

benefits.title: Immutable archive
benefits.body: WORM-style storage prevents tampering. Hash chains for cryptographic integrity verification.

faqs.question: What actions are logged?
faqs.answer: Every user action (login, view, edit, delete), admin change (role grant, account setting), workflow change (deploy, rollback, edit), data access (transcript view, recording download), API call.

faqs.question: How are logs delivered to my SIEM?
faqs.answer: Real-time webhook stream in JSON or CEF format. First-class connectors for Splunk, Datadog, Sumo Logic, Elastic. Generic webhook supports any SIEM.

faqs.question: How long are logs retained?
faqs.answer: Default 7 years. Configurable per compliance regime (HIPAA 6yr, SOX 7yr, GDPR variable). Immutable archive — logs can't be deleted or modified.

faqs.question: Can I query historical state?
faqs.answer: Yes — point-in-time queries reconstruct exact system state at any past timestamp. Useful for 'what did this workflow look like on March 5?' or breach investigations.

faqs.question: Is the audit log itself tamper-evident?
faqs.answer: Yes — WORM-style storage + hash chains. Each entry includes a cryptographic hash of the prior entry, making tampering detectable + provable.
